---
title: "Urban Water Utility Pump Scheduling and Anomaly-Aware Control"
domain: "water"
period: "2019-2024"
role: "Algorithm Lead / Co-Algorithm Lead"
problem: "Optimize pump scheduling and pressure compliance under demand uncertainty, sensor noise, and occasional data anomalies in SCADA-integrated environments."
method: "Forecast-optimize pipeline with demand prediction + MILP scheduling; anomaly-aware thresholding and strategy templates; hot-update and rollback support in deployment."
outcome: "Peak energy reduced (~10%), pump switching reduced (~20%), pressure compliance improved (~3%), and overflow risk reduced in resilience simulations."
status: "deployed"
confidentiality: "public-safe"
selected: true
toc: false
---

## Context

This project focuses on operational scheduling in urban water systems where multiple objectives must be balanced: energy cost, pressure compliance, equipment wear, and resilience under anomalies.

## Method

- Built demand forecasting and mixed-integer scheduling pipeline.
- Integrated pressure/flow constraints with deployment-oriented safeguards.
- Added anomaly-aware strategy templates with replay and audit support.
- Designed modular interfaces for SCADA integration and operator workflows.

## Outcome

- Reduced energy and switching costs while improving service-level metrics.
- Improved robustness against sensing anomalies and inflow disturbances.
- Produced reusable strategy templates for recurring operation patterns.

## Role

I led end-to-end algorithm design, deployment acceptance tests, and operator-facing runbook/training materials.
